info.name: Help PR — Admin & Franchise API (module-wise)
info.description:
Generated from help-pr-backend-staging Express routes (server.js). Folders align with admin dashboard modules.

**POST /api/getCount** — one endpoint for module dashboard cards. Send JSON `{ "type": "<module-key>" }`. Keys are defined in `controllers/count_controller.js` (`resolveCountType`). On API Gateway staging, `baseUrl` is the invoke URL **including** the stage, e.g. `https://dtvx6yflf1.execute-api.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/staging` (then paths are `/api/getCount`).

**Quote counts** — same logic as `GET /api/quote/getCounts` can be obtained with `type: "quote-management"`; optional body `franchise_id` filters by franchise (24-char ObjectId).

There is no dedicated Calendar REST API. Variables: `baseUrl`, `accessToken`, `id`, `chatId`, `userId`, `franchiseId`, `page`, `limit`, `keyword`.

Each request `url` includes `host`, `path`, and `query` (not only `raw`) so Postman shows the URL bar and Params tab correctly after import.

**My Franchise** routes are duplicated in folder **My Franchise** with example bodies; the same URLs also appear under User / Category / Service folders for reference.

variable.description: Optional for quote-management getCount and GET /api/quote/getCounts

item.name: 00 — Auth & OTP
item.description: Login, session, password recovery. OTP for verification flows.

item.name: 01 — Dashboard & Home
POST /api/getCount — by module (body.type)
item.description: All requests hit the same URL. Only `type` in the JSON body changes (plus optional `franchise_id` for quote-management). Aliases: `my_franchise`, `quote_management`, `quotes`, `order-payment` (same as financials).

request.description: Admin Service Management: category + service totals (for franchise admin user type 1, counts are scoped to requests from that franchise's users).
Quote dashboard cards: new, pending, accepted, success, failed (same buckets as GET /api/quote/getCounts).
Same as quote-management but scoped to one franchise when `franchise_id` is a valid ObjectId.
State / city / area totals and active vs inactive.
Users, employees, partners, document verification counts.
Alias in code: `order-payment`. Received vs pending order amounts (completed orders).
Partner payout aggregates from order services.
Franchise totals; for user type 1 filtered by admin_id.
Total expense rows for franchise-scoped user.
Expense category count for franchise.
Total content records.
Subscription plans and partner subscription totals (active/inactive).

item.description: Dashboard KPIs, aggregate counts, franchise home widgets. **POST /api/getCount** is the shared count API: use the nested folder and set `body.type` to the route segment (e.g. `my-franchise`, `service-management`, `quote-management`) — see `count_controller.js` typeMap.

item.name: My Franchise
item.description: Franchise admin **`/my-franchise`**: **Summary cards** — `POST /api/getCount` `{ "type": "my-franchise" }`. **Employees** — user APIs (`type=3`). **Areas** — franchise + area read/update. **Categories** / **Services** — `getAll` with `status` / `is_request`, getById, create-request, update-request, and full `PUT .../update/:id` for approved rows. JWT franchise admin (`type=1`). Variables: `franchiseId`, `areaId`, `id`, `categoryRequestId`, `categoryObjectId`.

item.name: POST getCount — my-franchise (Employees, Areas, Services, Categories)

request.description: Franchise admin card metrics. **Employees**: `total_employee`, `active_employee`, `inactive_employee`. **Areas**: `total_area`, `active_area`, `inactive_area`. **Categories**: `total_category`, `active_category`, `inactive_category`, `requested_category`. **Services**: `total_service`, `active_service`, `inactive_service`, `requested_service`. Pair list calls under **Categories** and **Services** folders (`GET .../getAll?status=...`) with these counts.
Use `type=3` for employees. Queries: `page`, `limit`, `keyword` or `search`, optional `is_active`, `is_blocked`. Franchise admin: list is limited to your franchise automatically.
Body is multipart/form-data per `user_routes` (field `image`). Set user `type` to 3 (employee) and `franchise_id` in form fields per `user_controller` rules.
PUT with multipart; same as User Management.

item.name: Get franchise by id (includes area_id used for Areas card)

request.description: Use your franchise `_id` as `franchiseId`. Area counts on My Franchise are derived from these area ids in `count_controller` (not a separate areas list API on this page).

item.name: Get area by id (detail for one area on franchise)

request.description: Optional: pass one `_id` from `franchise.area_id` into collection variable `areaId`.
Partial update allowed. `area_id` must be an array of ObjectIds when sent. See `updateFranchiseMiddleware`.

item.name: GET categories — all rows (no status filter)

request.description: Combine with client-side tabs or add `status` / `is_request` for server-side slices.
Approved categories that are active (`is_request=false`, `is_active=true`).
Approved categories that are inactive.
Same as `status=requested_categories`. Pending category requests (`is_request=true`).
Alias supported by `getCategoryStatusConfig` in `category_controller.js`.
Alternative to `status=requested`.
Uses same auth; optional query params per controller.

**Request body (JSON)** — `name`, `desc`, `image_url` (non-empty strings), `service_ids` (non-empty array of existing Service ObjectIds). Middleware sets `is_request=true`; controller sets `approval_status: "pending"`, `requested_by` = JWT user, `is_active` default false. Optional: `city_ids`, `state_ids` (arrays of ObjectId strings). Alternate: `multipart/form-data` with field `image` instead of `image_url` per `categoryCreateParser`.

**Response (200)** — `{ "success": true, "status": 200, "message": "Category created successfully.", "record": { ...Category document } }` with `is_request: true`, `approval_status: "pending"`.

**Response (409)** — duplicate name: `{ "success": false, "status": 409, "message": "Category name already exists." }`.

**Response (400)** — validation / invalid ObjectId in `service_ids` / cities / states.

response.name: 200 — category request created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "status": 200,
  "message": "Category created successfully.",
  "record": {
    "_id": "64a700000000000000000001",
    "name": "Requested Category",
    "desc": "Description for approvers",
    "category_id": "CAT-00001",
    "image_url": "https://example.com/cat.png",
    "services": ["64a700000000000000000010"],
    "city_ids": [],
    "state_ids": [],
    "is_active": false,
    "is_request": true,
    "approval_status": "pending",
    "rejection_reason": null,
    "requested_by": "64a700000000000000000099"
  }
}

request.description: Save changes to a request still in pending state. Use category `_id`.
Detail drawer / view action.
Full update for catalog categories (`is_request=false`) when your role allows. Multipart per `categoryUpdateParser`.
Franchise-scoped via `requested_by`.
Live services under franchise scope.
Inactive approved services.
Also accepts `status=requested` per `getServiceStatusConfig`.
Equivalent to `requested_services` for pending service requests.
Alternative filter for pending service requests.
Dropdown data for forms.

**Request body (JSON)** — Required by `createServiceRequestMiddleware`: `name`, `desc`, `image_url`, `category_id` (existing Category ObjectId), `tax`, `commission`, `minimum_deposit` (non-negative numbers), `payment_type` (e.g. `per_hour`, `per_day`, `per_month`, `per_consultancy`). Optional: `price` (defaults 0), `city_ids`, `state_ids` (arrays). Route sets `is_request=true` → `approval_status: "pending"`, `requested_by` = JWT user, `is_active` false by default.

**Response (200)** — `{ "success": true, "status": 200, "message": "Service created successfully.", "record": { ...Service } }`.

**Response (400)** — missing required field messages from middleware or invalid ids.

Alternate: `multipart/form-data` with `image` file per `serviceCreateParser` (still send other fields as form text).

response.name: 200 — service request created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "status": 200,
  "message": "Service created successfully.",
  "record": {
    "_id": "64a700000000000000000020",
    "name": "Requested Service",
    "desc": "Service description for approvers",
    "price": 0,
    "tax": 0,
    "commission": 0,
    "payment_type": "per_hour",
    "minimum_deposit": 0,
    "category_id": "64a700000000000000000001",
    "image_url": "https://example.com/svc.png",
    "is_active": false,
    "is_request": true,
    "approval_status": "pending",
    "requested_by": "64a700000000000000000099"
  }
}

request.description: Use service document `_id` for pending requests.
Detail / view action.
Full update for live services (`is_request=false`) when allowed. Multipart per `serviceUpdateParser`.

item.name: Franchise taxonomy (optional — super-admin creates)
item.description: Franchise-level category/service definitions; franchise admin/employee can list per `franchise_*_routes`.

item.name: Franchises
item.description: All franchise-related REST routes from `server.js`: **`/api/franchise`**, **`/api/franchise-category`**, **`/api/franchise-service`**. Use with `baseUrl` (e.g. API Gateway stage + host). Subscription / partner-subscription APIs stay under **03 — Franchise Management**.

item.name: Franchise
item.description: CRUD and import for franchise records (`franchise_routes.js`).

item.name: Franchise category
item.description: Franchise-scoped category definitions (`franchise_category_routes.js`). Create: super-admin/staff; list/get/update: franchise admin/employee and above.

item.name: Franchise service
item.description: Franchise-scoped service definitions (`franchise_service_management_routes.js`). Same role pattern as franchise category.

item.name: 02 — Location Management
item.description: State, city, area master data and imports.

item.name: 03 — Franchise Management
item.description: Franchises, franchise categories & services, plans and partner subscriptions (admin).

item.name: 04 — Service Management (catalog)
item.description: Global categories and services (admin catalog).

item.name: 05 — User Management

request.description: Use body form-data with field image if applicable.

item.description: Users, verification lists, dropdowns. create/update may use multipart (image).

request.description: Same five buckets (new, pending, accepted, success, failed) as **POST /api/getCount** with `{"type":"quote-management"}`. Optional query `franchise_id` filters by franchise.

item.description: Quotes lifecycle and conversion to order.

request.description: Payment provider webhook; usually no Bearer token.

item.description: Tax config, Razorpay webhook/callback, partner bank accounts.

item.description: Expense categories and expenses.

item.name: 10 — Reports & Analytics (export)
item.description: Excel/CSV style export endpoints (POST bodies per controller).

item.name: 11 — Partner Management

request.description: Admin **Add Partner** category dropdown. `POST /api/partner_category/franchiseActiveCategories`. JSON body: `franchise_id`. Response `records` = `franchise_category.active_categories` order. **requireBackoffice** JWT.

Admin **Add Partner** service dropdown. `POST /api/partner_service/franchiseCategoryServices`. Intersection of **category.services** and **franchise_service.active_services**. Set `categoryObjectId` from `_id` returned by franchiseActiveCategories.
Route has no authMiddleware in code; add if your deployment differs.

item.description: Partner services (admin + partner self-service), documents.

item.name: 12 — Settings (documents & uploads)

request.description: multipart/form-data, field files (max 5).

item.description: Generic documents and file upload helpers.

request.description: No authMiddleware on create in routes.
